mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive forward returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving corporate earnings expectations and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward windows across 5-year rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0% month-over-month during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and impending inflation, prompting equity market sell-offs over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns exceed 60% frequency when INDPRO growth surpasses 1.0% during late-cycle phases, invalidating the capacity constraint sell-off mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real purchasing power and compress future corporate margins, generating negative short-term equity returns as markets price in inflation drag.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 55% of observations, negating the inflation drag effect.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and stagnant unemployment rates indicates labor hoarding and weakening labor demand quality, forecasting negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The specified payroll-unemployment divergence yields positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, disproving the labor hoarding risk premium.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized signals demand stagnation that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during post-shock recovery phases indicates fragile supply chains, triggering immediate negative equity returns as investors discount near-term output gaps.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns fail to materialize in more than 55% of observations when INDPRO declines during post-shock recovery periods.
